title: Pruning
text: Home * Search * Selectivity * Pruning Samuel Bak - Under the Trees [1] Pruning , (as opposed to reductions ) a name for every heuristic that removes completely certain branches of the search tree , assuming they have no bearing to the search result. Alpha-Beta may be considered as backward pruning, because we found a refutation after searching [2] . Forward pruning always involves some risks to overlook something, with influence on the root score. Some pruning techniques rely on a specialized, but reduced search, others rely only on static move properties. Shannon Type B programs only consider some plausible mores, but all other moves are pruned. Contents 1 Backward pruning 2 Forward pruning techniques 2.1 Returning Beta 2.2 Returning Alpha 2.3 Skipping Moves 3 See also 4 Publications 4.1 1960 ... 4.2 1970 ... 4.3 1980 ... 4.4 1990 ... 4.5 2000 ... 4.6 2010 ... 5 Forum Posts 5.1 1995 ... 5.2 2000 ... 5.3 2005 ... 5.4 2010 ... 5.5 2015 ... 5.6 2020 ... 6 External Links 7 References Backward pruning [ edit ] sound pruning, not affecting the search result Mate Distance Pruning Forward pruning techniques [ edit ] Forward pruning techniques are either applied at expected Cut-Nodes or All-Nodes and return either beta as lower bound or alpha as upper bound . The none recursive ProbCut is applied at both types of none PV-nodes . Rather than immediately returning alpha at strong expected All-Nodes after a reduced or quiescence search , pruning techniques near the horizon skip moves, which are very unlikely to exceed alpha, or in case of the quiescence search itself, all (most) quiet moves as well as losing captures . Returning Beta [ edit ] at expected Cut-Nodes : Null Move Pruning - if reduced nullmove search fails high, return beta Multi-Cut - if a reduced, specialized search has C cut-offs from N tried moves, return beta Enhanced Forward Pruning , also applicable at expected All-Nodes ProbCut Reverse Futility Pruning Standing Pat in Quiescence Search Returning Alpha [ edit ] or below at expected All-Nodes or expected Cut-Nodes which turn out to become All-Nodes: after trying width[ply] moves in Shannon Type-B programs, return alpha Sibling Prediction Pruning - return alpha, or depending on the implementation, only skip the move ProbCut Uncertainty Cut-Offs Skipping Moves [ edit ] likely at All-Nodes : Bobby's Strategic Quiescence Search Futility Pruning Extended Futility Pruning Deep Futility Pruning Move Count Based Pruning AEL-Pruning Delta Pruning in Quiescence Search History Leaf Pruning Parity Pruning See also [ edit ] Extensions Razoring Reductions Late Move Reductions History Pruning Pruning in Nimzo 2.2.1 Quiescence Search Shannon Type B Strategy Publications [ edit ] 1960 ...
